info.title: Collision Engineers Parser
info.description: Custom connector for the cedocumentmapper_v2 parser Function. POST a base64 instruction document to /parse and receive the settled 12-field EVA extraction (with per-field value/confidence/source), plus the Case-identity vrm/reference surfaced separately. POST inbound email metadata to /classify-email and receive the deterministic Phase-8 triage label (category/subtype/signals + body_vrm/body_caseref). Feature gating is enforced in the calling flow, not here.

post.description: Decode a base64 document (PLAIN string param — NEVER format:byte / x-ms-media-kind:File), extract the 12-field EVA payload, and return it with field-level provenance plus Case-identity vrm/reference.

200.description: Parsed result (extraction may be incomplete; see issues).

400.description: Bad input (missing/invalid document — e.g. not single base64 (bad_base64) — filename, or JSON).

422.description: The document itself is unreadable (corrupt/truncated/not a real document, or a double-encoded payload whose single decode is not a parseable document). A client problem; route the case to review, do not retry.

post.description: Run an inbound email's already-decoded fields through the deterministic Phase-8 triage classifier and return the operator's label (category/subtype) with the exact signals that fired plus any Case/PO or registration found in the body. PURE: keyword / phrase / regex only — no Dataverse, no network, no AI. The open-Case lookup (does body_caseref / body_vrm hit an open Case?) stays in the calling flow, exactly as Case-identity stays out of /parse. Even a malformed request returns a safe 'other' label so a call always routes to a person.

200.description: Triage label with the fired signals.

400.description: Bad input (body not JSON / a field has the wrong type). The body still carries a safe 'other' label so the caller parses one schema.

document.description: Base64-encoded document bytes (.pdf/.docx/.doc/.eml/.msg). DO NOT add `format: byte` or `x-ms-media-kind: File` to this param: the gateway then re-encodes the base64 a SECOND time and the parser returns 422 document_unreadable (a burst of CS Parse 422s broke intake when format:byte was added 2026-06-19). The Power Automate flow passes the RAW base64 string `@triggerBody()?['instructionBytesB64']` — NOT `@base64ToBinary(...)`: with this plain-string connector base64ToBinary feeds the gateway BINARY and it returns HTTP 400 (proven live 2026-06-20: test34 -> 400 -> Exceptions; the same doc posts 200 directly to /api/parse). The gateway's encoding DRIFTS with connector state, so the parser `_decode_document` is TOLERANT (peels a redundant 2nd base64 layer, logging each recovery) — THAT is the load-bearing safeguard, not the flow's encoding choice. See memory `powerplatform-connector-base64-double-encode`.

filename.description: Original filename WITH extension (selects the parser reader).

provider_hint.description: Optional provider id or name to bias provider detection.

FieldCell.description: One EVA field value with provenance.

source.description: Extraction rule id / provenance label, or 'absent' when the parser did not supply this field.

Extraction.description: The settled 12-field EVA extraction. Keys are emitted in contract order.

Audit.description: Audit case-type signal — surfaced SEPARATELY (like vrm/reference), NEVER part of the EVA payload. Content-derived by the parser; the envelope shape is stable, so this is always present (signals may be empty).

source.description: Provenance label for the audit decision (e.g. 'instruction_text').

vrm.description: Case-identity (correlation/dedup). NOT part of the EVA payload.

reference.description: Case-identity (correlation/dedup). NOT part of the EVA payload.

audit.description: Audit case-type signal. NOT part of the EVA payload.

ClassifyEmailRequest.description: Inbound email metadata for the deterministic Phase-8 triage classifier. All fields optional — a missing field is treated as empty/absent. The body may be HTML or plain text; the Function strips HTML server-side before scanning.

body.description: The email body (HTML or plain text; HTML is stripped server-side before keyword / registration scanning).

sender_domain.description: The sender's domain (the part after '@'), lower-cased by the caller.

provider_match_state.description: The calling flow's sender-domain match result: 'one' | 'none' | 'ambiguous'.

attachment_kinds.description: The kinds of attachment on the email, e.g. ["instruction", "image"]. An empty list / absent means no attachments.

has_attachments.description: Whether the email carried any attachment.

in_reply_to.description: The RFC-5322 In-Reply-To header, if available. The authoritative reply signal — when present it marks the email a reply (about EXISTING work) even with a plain subject, strengthening detection beyond the 'RE:' subject fallback. A 'FW:'/'FWD:' forward is never treated as a reply.

references.description: The RFC-5322 References header, if available. Treated like In-Reply-To: when non-empty it marks the email a reply.

ClassifyEmailResponse.description: The triage label. The same shape is returned for success and for a bad request — on a bad request the label is the safe catch-all 'other' so the caller always parses one schema and routes to a person.

confidence.description: Coarse confidence band 0.0–1.0 (lets a later gated AI pass target only the low-confidence rows).

signals.description: The exact rule ids / phrases that fired, for explainability (joined into the triage row's signals memo). Includes the token 'reply' when the email is a reply.

is_reply.description: The email is a REPLY in an existing thread (not a forward) — derived from the In-Reply-To / References headers when supplied, else a leading 'RE:' subject. The orchestrator keys on this to run the EXISTING-Case lookup (by body_caseref then body_vrm) instead of minting a new Case: a reply about work we already do is suppressed out of receiving_work into query_existing_work unless the sender added a NEW work phrase.

body_vrm.description: The first vehicle registration found in subject+body, or empty. The caller runs the open-Case lookup; the classifier never auto-links.

body_caseref.description: The first Case/PO found in subject+body, or empty. The caller links by Case/PO first, registration only as a fallback; the classifier never auto-links.

contract_version.description: The classifier contract tag (distinct from the /parse EVA contract tag so a consumer can tell the two envelopes apart).

apiKeyHeader.name: x-functions-key
apiKeyHeader.description: Azure Functions host/function key, stored on the custom connection. Never embedded in this definition.

x-ms-connector-metadata.propertyValue: FUNCTION-level auth: the connector sends the function key as x-functions-key. The API gateway additionally fronts the endpoint; do not expose the Function host directly.
